msg: PLANNED iter002_stmatrix_wn192: Fix 8-way shared memory bank conflicts in output store by using stmatrix instead of direct store, increase WARP_N from 128 to 192
PLANNED iter003_ws1p1c_wn192: Switch to 1p1c warpspec (fewer consumers), increase WARP_N to 192, reduce TILE_M to 64 for better occupancy
PLANNED iter004_ws1p2c_wn192: Keep 1p2c warpspec (which was better than 1p1c), increase WARP_N from 128 to 192 to improve N-dim coverage
PLANNED iter005_ws1p3c_s4: Structural change: 1p3c warpspec (3 consumers for M=192 coverage) + 4-stage pipeline for better latency hiding
PLANNED iter006_ws1p2c_wn256: Push WARP_N to max 256 (from 192), keep 1p2c+3-stage which was best config so far
PLANNED iter007_nonws_wn256: Structural change: non-warpspec design to reduce barrier stalls, keep WARP_N=256 for good N-coverage
PLANNED iter008_ws1p2c_wn224_s3: WARP_N=224 (between 192 and 256), 3-stage pipeline for better latency hiding vs 2-stage
PLANNED iter009_ws1p3c_wn192: 1p3c warpspec with WARP_N=192 to reduce register pressure (167->~140 regs) and shared memory, improving occupancy
PLANNED iter010_ws1p2c_wn256_s3: Keep WARP_N=256 (best so far), increase pipeline stages from 2 to 3 to hide memory latency
PLANNED iter010_ws1p2c_wn256_tk32: WARP_N=256 with TILE_K=32 (half K-dim) to reduce shared mem pressure and potentially increase CTA count
PLANNED iter011_persis_wn128_s3: Persistent kernel with WARP_N=128, 3 stages, 114 CTAs - amortize launch overhead, reduce barrier stalls
PLANNED iter012_persis_wn256_s3: Persistent kernel with WARP_N=256, 3 stages - larger N-tile with persistent kernel benefits
PLANNED iter013_persis_wn256_tm64: Persistent with WARP_N=256, TILE_M=64, 1p1c - single consumer reduces register pressure
PLANNED iter014_persis_1p3c_wn192: Persistent 1p3c with TILE_M=192, WARP_N=192, 2 stages - 3 consumers for better parallelism
PLANNED iter015_persis_wn256_lb: Persistent WARP_N=256 with __launch_bounds__(384, 2) to increase occupancy via nvcc register pressure
PLANNED iter016_persis_wn256_col: Persistent WARP_N=256 with column-major tile traversal for better L2 cache reuse
PLANNED iter017_persis_col_s3: Column-major persistent with WARP_N=192 and 3 stages for better latency hiding
PLANNED iter018_persis_wn256_hil: Persistent WARP_N=256 with swizzled tile ordering (XOR pattern) for better L2 reuse
PLANNED iter019_persis_col_1p3c: Column-major persistent with 1p3c and WARP_N=128, TILE_M=192 for more consumer parallelism
PLANNED iter020_persis_col_tm192: Column-major persistent WARP_N=128, TILE_M=192, 1p3c - more M coverage
PLANNED iter021_persis_col_reg128: Persistent col-major WARP_N=128 with maxrregcount=128 to increase occupancy from 14% to ~30%
PLANNED iter022_persis_col_lb2: Persistent col-major WARP_N=256 with launch_bounds(384,2) to hint 2 blocks/SM
PLANNED iter023_persis_rowmaj: Persistent row-major traversal with WARP_N=256 - different L2 access pattern
PLANNED iter024_persis_col_s4: 4-stage pipeline with WARP_N=256 col-major - hide memory latency better
PLANNED iter025_persis_1p3c_tm192: 1p3c warp spec with TILE_M=192 WARP_N=192 - 3 consumers for better compute overlap
PLANNED iter026_static_wn256: Non-persistent static CTA launch with WARP_N=256 TILE_M=128 - more CTAs for occupancy
PLANNED iter027_persis_wn224: Persistent WARP_N=224 col-major - untested N-tile size between 192 and 256
PLANNED iter028_persis_regctrl: Add maxrregcount=96 nvcc flag to reduce register pressure and improve occupancy
PLANNED iter029_persis_reg200: Try maxrregcount=200 to allow more registers while still reducing pressure
PLANNED iter030_persis_tm64_s3: TILE_M=64 with 3-stage pipeline and WARP_N=256 to trade M-parallelism for better latency hiding
PLANNED iter031_kloop_unroll2: Aggressive K-loop unrolling with double buffered MMA loads - unroll inner WARP_K loop 2x and overlap mma.load with previous mma.row.row
PLANNED iter032_persis_wn192_s3: Persistent kernel with WARP_N=192 and 3 pipeline stages - more stages to hide WGMMA latency, smaller N reduces SMEM pressure
PLANNED iter033_persis_1p1c_wn256: Persistent 1p1c warpspec with WARP_N=256 TILE_M=64 - reduce threads/CTA to 256 (vs 384) for higher occupancy while keeping large N-tile
PLANNED iter034_persis_tk128: Persistent 1p2c with TILE_K=128 - larger K-tile reduces loop iterations from 8 to 4, reducing loop overhead for small K=512
PLANNED iter035_persis_reg140: Reduce maxrregcount from 160 to 140 - lower register pressure may allow higher occupancy on H800
PLANNED iter036_persis_wn128_s3_colmaj: Persistent 1p2c with WARP_N=128, 3 stages, column-major traversal - more stages for latency hiding, smaller N fits SMEM, column-major for L2 locality
PLANNED iter037_persis_fastmath: Add --use_fast_math to nvcc flags - enables faster math operations that may reduce instruction latency
PLANNED iter038_persis_swiz64: Change swizzle from 128 to 64 with TILE_K=32 - different memory access pattern may help L2 cache behavior
PLANNED iter039_persis_132sms: Increase NUM_SMS from 114 to 132 (full H100) - more CTAs may help load balance and reduce tail effects
PLANNED iter040_persis_rowmaj_opt: Try row-major tile traversal (block_m varies faster) - may benefit from different L2 cache reuse pattern for this tall-K shape
PLANNED iter041_persis_tm192_wn192: TILE_M=192 WARP_N=192 with 1p3c - larger M-tile uses 3 consumer warpgroups, balanced M/N tiles
PLANNED iter042_persis_dlcmca: Add -Xptxas -dlcm=ca to force cache all loads - may improve memory latency hiding
PLANNED iter043_persis_mmaop: Use mma.op form instead of mma.row.row - may produce different optimized WGMMA instruction sequence
PLANNED iter044_persis_wn240: WARP_N=240 - between 256 and 224, may hit a sweet spot for this shape
PLANNED iter045_persis_tm256_wn256: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=256 with 1p4c - maximum tile size with 4 consumer warpgroups
PLANNED iter046_persis_wn248: WARP_N=248 - fine-tuning between 240 and 256 for potential sweet spot
PLANNED iter047_persis_tm192_wn192_s2: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=192 and STAGES=2 - try balanced larger tiles with reduced SMEM
PLANNED iter048_persis_tm192_wn256_s2: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=256 and STAGES=2 - keep larger TILE_M, try wider N-tile
PLANNED iter049_persis_tm192_wn224_s2: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=224 and STAGES=2 - intermediate N between 192 and 256
PLANNED iter050_persis_tm192_wn192_s3: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=192 and STAGES=3 - increase pipeline depth with smaller tiles
PLANNED iter051_persis_tm192_wn160_s3: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=160 and STAGES=3 - smaller N-tile enables 3 stages
PLANNED iter052_persis_tm192_wn128_1p3c: TILE_M=192 with WARP_N=128 and 1p3c - more consumers, smaller N-tile, STAGES=2
PLANNED iter053_persis_tm256_wn192: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=192 and STAGES=2 - even larger M-tile
PLANNED iter054_persis_tm256_wn256: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=256 and STAGES=2 - try maximal tile sizes
PLANNED iter055_persis_tm256_wn224: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=224 and STAGES=2 - larger N but within SMEM
PLANNED iter056_persis_tm256_wn200: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=200 and STAGES=2 - finding max N within SMEM limit
PLANNED iter057_persis_tm256_wn208: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=208 and STAGES=2 - slightly larger N within SMEM
PLANNED iter058_persis_tm256_wn200_tk32: TILE_M=256 with WARP_N=200, TILE_K=32 and STAGES=3 - smaller K for more stages
PLANNED iter059_persis_tm256_wn200_reg128: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with maxrregcount=128 - try higher occupancy
PLANNED iter060_persis_tm256_wn200_reg180: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with maxrregcount=180 - more registers
PLANNED iter061_persis_tm320_wn160: TILE_M=320 with WARP_N=160 - try even larger M tile
PLANNED iter062_persis_tm256_wn200_1p1c: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with 1p1c topology - fewer threads per CTA
PLANNED iter063_persis_tm256_wn200_reg170: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with maxrregcount=170
PLANNED iter064_persis_tm256_wn200_reg165: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with maxrregcount=165 - between 160 and 170
PLANNED iter065_persis_tm256_wn200_reg175: TILE_M=256 WARP_N=200 with maxrregcount=175 - above optimal 170
